en.stage1Text1: Good morning Postbot. We have some letters that need delivering.
en.stage1Text2: It looks like some of the house numbers on this street have fallen off. Let's help the residents by putting the numbers back where they belong. I'm sure they'll leave us a few tips for helping!
en.stage1Text3: Notice how the houses are lined up from 10 to 20. Just like a number line, the numbers get bigger as we move to the right. These houses only use even numbers, so each house is two numbers higher than the one before it.
en.stage1Text4: You can move around the WASD or the arrow keys. Let’s go pick up a number and place it where it should go. Let's have a look down the street.
en.stage1Text5: Here's a house number. Pick it up with the E key and place it on the correct house.
en.stage1Text6: It looks like house number 10 already has a house number. Look at the number line at the top of the screen to help you find the correct house. When you think you have found it, press E again on the house to place it.
en.stage1Text7: Not quite. Remember, the numbers get bigger as you move to the right along the number line. Try again.
en.stage1Text8: Well done! This house now has its number plaque in place. Lets do the same with the final 3 houses.
en.stage1Text9: Excellent work, Postbot! Every house has its number back. Now the post can finally be delivered.
en.stage1Text10: We have a problem though, the addresses on the post are all wrong. We need to round up or down the address number on the post to the nearest 10 to find the correct house.
en.stage1Text11: This first letter is addressed to number 11. We don't have a house numbered 11 on this street. So we need to round the house number on the letter, up or down, to the nearest 10 and deliver it to that address.
en.stage1Text12: When we round to the nearest 10, numbers from 10 to 14 round down to 10. Numbers from 15 to 19 round up to 20. Since 11 is closer to 10 than 20, we deliver this letter to house number 10.
en.stage1Text13: Well done! This letter was supposed to be addressed to house number 10. The owner of the house will be very happy they got their mail. You're on your own for the next 2 letters. Good Luck.
en.stage1Text14: Are you sure this letter gets delivered here?  Remember ask yourself which multiple of 10 the address is closest to.  Maybe have another look and try again.
en.stage1Text16: Fantastic. You delivered the letter to the correct address.
en.stage1Text17: This is Ethyl's house. She's asked us to be careful and not knock over any of her prized flamingos. If we do, she won't leave us a tip. So let's be extra careful around her garden!
en.stage1Text18: Congratulations, Postbot! That’s all our letters delivered.  Let’s look at how much you earned today.
en.stage1Text19: Wow, good work. We need to do one final task though. The robot post office always rounds wages to the nearest 10 before paying their workers.
en.stage1Text20: Move the slider on the wages number line or type the value into the text box above.
en.stage1Text21: Are you sure? Maybe have a look at the amount in your wages and try again.
en.stage1Text22: Excellent job! Now you’ve been paid for today’s work. The rest of the day is yours!  I'll see you tomorrow for your paper route.
